notes: phi <-> psi == (phi -> psi) and (psi -> phi) expands the biconditional via the conditional laws (thm:conditional-equivalence-laws); the second form (phi and psi) or (not phi and not psi) additionally uses basic boolean/distributive manipulation, which is reachable from the conditional + foundational laws, so not a missing direct edge. == is def:logical-equivalence.
